category_title: Lower-achieving students with reactive study habits
student_description: This student struggles with last-minute cramming, neglecting sleep, and taking excessive, unstructured breaks (relying on them 92% of the time). They react emotionally to poor grades rather than adjusting their habits. They uniquely require anonymous peer data to evaluate their habits without feeling forced to confess, alongside highly specific parameters for break management and study timelines.

criteria.rationale: Using anonymous data prevents defensiveness and allows the student to objectively evaluate their habits without feeling forced to confess.
criteria.check: The tutor uses or references anonymous peer data (e.g., 'everyday data' or high-achiever habits) to prompt self-reflection, explicitly avoiding forcing the student to confess their own bad habits.

criteria.rationale: Because the student leans on breaks 92% of the time, they need concrete time boundaries to prevent breaks from derailing their study sessions.
criteria.check: The tutor provides specific, numerical parameters for break times (e.g., 5-15 minutes after an hour of studying) rather than general advice to 'take breaks'.

criteria.rationale: Sharing personal mistakes builds relatedness and lowers the student's affective filter, making them more receptive to redirecting their shallow study methods.
criteria.check: The tutor shares a personal mistake or vulnerability regarding study habits to model better strategies and build peer connection.
